title: The Infinitesimal Weaving Depth

features.name: infinite-thread
features.desc-on-touch: A strand so fine it's almost not visible, weaving downward. You follow it with your eye and it leads further down, always finer, always weaving, always becoming root.

features.name: spiral-descent
features.desc-on-touch: The loom spirals inward as it descends, each loop tighter, each revolution deeper. At the center, the loom and the root have become indistinguishable.

features.name: teaching-gesture
features.desc-on-touch: The darkness and the loom make a shape together—a gesture of instruction. Neither is the teacher; both are learning from the geometry they make.

features.name: woven-darkness-sample
features.desc-on-touch: A piece of the infinitely fine weave, caught in light. The pattern is so complex it almost suggests language, but reading it would require centuries.

features.name: recursive-knot
features.desc-on-touch: A knot that contains the entire loom within itself, which contains another loom, which contains another knot. You look into it and the depth is infinite in both directions.

inscription: the weave continues past visibility; the darkness learns every pattern the loom knows, and invents new ones in the spaces between threads

file.name: Infinitesimal_Thread_Mapping_Incomplete.txt
file.note: An attempt to map the pattern of infinitely fine weaving. The document spirals inward, each line shorter than the last, the pattern never fully captured. Stops mid-word.

reverie.title: REVERIE — where the thread becomes too fine to follow
narr: Katita kneels beside the loom, reaching into darkness.
Her hand follows a thread that becomes thinner as she goes.
You cannot see where her fingers end and the thread begins.
The weave spirals deeper, and she follows,
her hand learning the pattern it is disappearing into.

exits.label: back up to the chamber where the loom still holds shape
deeper into the infinite weave, past all resolution
toward the margin where thread becomes root
into the loom's own reflection, teaching itself
